added_lemmas.statement: Let r_*=(1/2)min{r_0,x_{0,1},1-x_{0,1},...,x_{0,d},1-x_{0,d}}. There is c_TV>0 such that for every P,Q in the frozen observational Holder CATE class, with Delta=|tau_P(x_0)-tau_Q(x_0)|, TV(P,Q)>=c_TV Delta^{1+d/gamma}. Hence, for every eta in (0,1), any central-(epsilon_n,delta_n)-DP causal two-point lower-bound argument that certifies output indistinguishability through n{exp(epsilon_n)-1+delta_n}TV(P,Q)<=eta must have Delta<=C_eta(n epsilon_n)^{-gamma/(gamma+d)}. In particular, if q=alpha+beta<gamma and n epsilon_n>1, the ordinary two-point/TV-contraction method cannot establish a lower bound of the larger formal order (n epsilon_n)^{-q/(q+d)}; at n epsilon_n=1 the two powers coincide.
added_lemmas.justification: This is the primary causal-privacy hardness result: it delimits the full ordinary two-point proof route over arbitrary admissible causal-law pairs.
added_lemmas.gap: Private pointwise-regression lower bounds do not provide this arbitrary-pair obstruction for an observational CATE class with two outcome arms, varying propensity, and varying design density.
added_lemmas.consumer: A converse for a nuisance-privacy branch with alpha+beta<gamma must use a comparison method beyond the displayed two-point TV contraction, such as a genuinely multi-hypothesis or fuzzy construction.
added_lemmas.proof_tex:
Put C_*={x:||x-x_0||_infinity<=r_*}. For a in {0,1}, define pi_{1,P}=pi_P, pi_{0,P}=1-pi_P and w_{a,P}(x)=p_P(x)pi_{a,P}(x); define pi_{a,Q} and w_{a,Q} analogously. Let K^P_{a,x} and K^Q_{a,x} be regular conditional laws of Y given (A=a,X=x), and write d_a(x)=mu_{a,P}(x)-mu_{a,Q}(x). By ass:overlap and ass:local-density, for every x in C_* and both arms, w_{a,P}(x),w_{a,Q}(x)>=c_0:=e_0f_0.

We first prove a finite-measure inequality. Let K,L be probability laws on [-1,1] with means m_K,m_L, let s,t>=c_0, and put nu=sK-tL. Since the total mass of nu is s-t and |y|<=1,

 s|m_K-m_L| <= |integral y dnu|+|s-t||m_L| <= 2||nu||_{TV,var},

where ||.||_{TV,var} denotes the total-variation norm of a finite signed measure. Therefore ||sK-tL||_{TV,var}>=(c_0/2)|m_K-m_L|. Apply this inequality pointwise with (s,t,K,L)=(w_{a,P}(x),w_{a,Q}(x),K^P_{a,x},K^Q_{a,x}). Disintegrating P and Q over the two disjoint treatment arms gives

 TV(P,Q)=(1/2)||P-Q||_{TV,var}
 >=(c_0/4) integral_{C_*}(|d_0(x)|+|d_1(x)|)dx
 >=(c_0/4) integral_{C_*}|tau_P(x)-tau_Q(x)|dx.

The last inequality is the triangle inequality because tau_P-tau_Q=d_1-d_0. By lem:holder-point-l1-interpolation, the last integral is at least c_H Delta^{1+d/gamma}. Thus TV(P,Q)>=c_TV Delta^{1+d/gamma}, where c_TV=c_0c_H/4. Notice that no equality of the two design densities or propensities was used.

Now put b_n=exp(epsilon_n)-1+delta_n. By lem:dp-output-tv-contraction, every central-DP mechanism satisfies TV(L_{P^n}(M_n),L_{Q^n}(M_n))<=n b_n TV(P,Q). If the stated two-point route certifies n b_n TV(P,Q)<=eta, the preceding lower bound implies

 Delta <= (eta/c_TV)^{gamma/(gamma+d)}(n b_n)^{-gamma/(gamma+d)}.

The frozen privacy ranges give delta_n<=n^{-2}<=epsilon_n and 0<epsilon_n<=1. Hence epsilon_n<=b_n<=(e-1)epsilon_n+delta_n<=e epsilon_n, and consequently Delta<=C_eta(n epsilon_n)^{-gamma/(gamma+d)}. Finally, q<gamma implies q/(q+d)<gamma/(gamma+d). Since n epsilon_n>=1, (n epsilon_n)^{-q/(q+d)} is strictly larger than the gamma power when n epsilon_n>1 and equal to it when n epsilon_n=1. Therefore a converse of the formal q-private order cannot be obtained by this ordinary two-point/TV-contraction certificate. The conclusion concerns that proof method only; it is not an impossibility theorem for fuzzy mixtures, hypercubes, or other private comparison inequalities.

added_lemmas.statement: When beta=gamma, the sharp full-class central-DP CATE rate in lem:equal-smoothness-sharp-private-rate is inherited, at the level of rates, from one-server private pointwise regression: the upper mechanism is the clipped difference of two armwise private local-polynomial regressions, while the causal lower family fixes mu_0=0 and a constant overlapping propensity and embeds a single pointwise-regression subproblem in mu_1. Overlap and the two-arm structure affect only constants.
added_lemmas.justification: This comparison states exactly which sharp calibration is inherited from private regression and prevents presenting the beta=gamma rate as a causally distinct rate result.
added_lemmas.gap: The causal content at beta=gamma is the valid embedding and full-class calibration, not a new exponent beyond private pointwise regression.
added_lemmas.consumer: Readers can separate the inherited sharp calibration from the genuinely causal two-point obstruction and the unresolved nuisance-privacy frontier.
added_lemmas.proof_tex:
The achievability proof in lem:private-local-polynomial-upper-bound forms, for each a in {0,1}, a privatized local polynomial estimate of mu_{a,P}(x_0) from the arm-a weighted moments and releases the clipped difference of the two intercept estimates. Its uniform error is the sum of two fixed-constant copies of

 h^beta+(nh^d)^{-1/2}+1/(n epsilon_n h^d).

When beta=gamma, optimizing this expression gives n^{-gamma/(2gamma+d)} vee (n epsilon_n)^{-gamma/(gamma+d)}. Thus the upper exponent is exactly the one-server private pointwise-regression exponent; differencing two arms changes only the multiplicative constant.

For the converse, the explicit family in lem:causal-oracle-private-lower-bound holds the covariate density fixed, takes the constant propensity pi=e_0, sets mu_0=0 under both hypotheses, and changes only mu_1 by a localized gamma-Holder bump. It is an admissible causal family because potential outcomes are generated before treatment, treatment is conditionally independent of them given X, and Y=Y(A). On this subfamily tau_P(x_0)=mu_{1,P}(x_0), so estimating the CATE contains the single pointwise-regression problem as a subproblem. The constant treatment probability thins informative observations only by the fixed factor e_0, and replacement adjacency of the full observational sample remains replacement adjacency on this embedded subproblem; these facts change constants but not either exponent. Lem:equal-smoothness-sharp-private-rate combines the matching bounds. Hence the beta=gamma sharp rate is regression-inherited at the rate level, notwithstanding that the lower family is written as a valid observational causal law.

open_obligations.what_is_open: For beta<gamma, determine the sharp central-(epsilon_n,delta_n)-DP minimax absolute risk over the full locally positive-density observational Holder class. This includes determining whether its privacy-free limit is r_n^CATE on that full class and whether the formal (alpha+beta)-privacy power, or a different nuisance/private-Gram power, is an attainable and necessary regime.
open_obligations.obstruction: The certified full-class lower and upper bounds have different smoothness exponents. Complete central-DP achievability at the formal higher-order objective would require privacy or global stability for nuisance pilots, the local Gram estimate and inverse, first-order scores, and the second-order fold, together with control of the KBRW random-design Gram/density third-order remainder under only the frozen local density floor. Those controls are not consequences of the frozen assumptions. For the converse, thm:causal-dp-two-point-barrier rules out obtaining the larger q=alpha+beta<gamma privacy separation through the ordinary two-point certificate n{exp(epsilon_n)-1+delta_n}TV(P,Q)<=eta. A same-class converse therefore needs a comparison beyond this route, for example a positive-density multi-hypothesis or fuzzy construction with a proved approximate-DP tracing inequality. Neither achievability nor necessity of the q branch is presently proved.
open_obligations.attempted: On the upper side, foldwise accounting shows that privatizing only the terminal score does not protect records entering nuisance or Gram computations; separately privatizing those objects creates error terms omitted from E_n^alg, while treating them as nonprivate internal computations violates central DP. The explicit armwise private local-polynomial estimator gives the uniform beta-smooth upper endpoint. On the lower side, the localized causal two-point family gives the nonprivate gamma branch and the gamma privacy branch. Extending the interpolation argument to arbitrary design densities and propensities yields the theorem-level two-point barrier. A positive-floor paired propensity/outcome microcell program still lacks a mixture-divergence cancellation and a matching approximate-DP hypercube inequality, so asserting its q-scale behavior would assume the crux.
open_obligations.partial_result: The primary established result is thm:causal-dp-two-point-barrier: for arbitrary P,Q in the frozen causal class, TV(P,Q)>=c|tau_P(x_0)-tau_Q(x_0)|^{1+d/gamma}, so no two-point argument using the displayed DP TV-contraction certificate can establish more than the gamma privacy separation (n epsilon_n)^{-gamma/(gamma+d)}. This is a proof-method hardness theorem, not a claim against fuzzy or multi-hypothesis methods. As secondary calibration, lem:certified-private-cate-bracket proves c{n^{-gamma/(2gamma+d)} vee (n epsilon_n)^{-gamma/(gamma+d)}} <= R_n^DP <= C{n^{-beta/(2beta+d)} vee (n epsilon_n)^{-beta/(beta+d)}}. The bracket is sharp when beta=gamma and when 1<=n epsilon_n<=C_0. Lem:equal-smoothness-regression-inheritance records that the beta=gamma exponent is inherited from one-server private pointwise regression: the upper bound differences two armwise private regressions and the causal lower family embeds one regression subproblem, with overlap affecting only constants. The formal optimized value n^{-kappa} vee (n epsilon_n)^{-min(gamma,alpha+beta)/(min(gamma,alpha+beta)+d)} remains algebraic only. The full beta<gamma frontier, including achievability and converse for any nuisance-privacy branch and the privacy-free full-positive-density limit, remains open.
